primary_diagnosis.name: Deep laceration of the forearm requiring surgical wound closure

pathophysiology_summary: A kitchen knife has caused a full-thickness laceration through skin and subcutaneous tissue on the volar forearm, with venous bleeding controlled partially by direct pressure. Preserved full range of motion against resistance and normal sensation are reassuring for intact tendons and major nerves, but definitive wound exploration by a clinician is still required to confirm this and to formally close the wound. Wounds of this depth and gape heal better, with less scarring and lower infection risk, when cleaned and sutured within the 'golden period' (generally within 6-8 hours, longer for facial/well-vascularised areas), making timely in-person assessment important, though this is not an immediately life-threatening emergency given the patient's stable vital signs and controlled bleeding.

differential.evidence_basis: Description of a deep, gaping wound with visible fat and ongoing bleeding.
Must be actively excluded by checking motor/sensory function, though this patient's description of normal movement and sensation is reassuring.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Motor/sensory function must be actively checked to help distinguish a simple laceration from one with deeper structural injury.

differential.diagnosis: Deep laceration requiring formal closure, without evidence of tendon/nerve/major vascular injury
differential.evidence_basis: Preserved motor and sensory function (DI-003) is reassuring, and bleeding partially controlled by pressure (DI-002) suggests no major arterial injury.

differential_progression.scoring_note: By this stage, urgent (same-day/within hours) in-person wound closure should be recommended, without necessarily requiring an ambulance.

differential.diagnosis: Deep laceration of the forearm requiring surgical wound closure
differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis requiring prompt in-person wound exploration, irrigation, and suturing, plus tetanus prophylaxis review.

differential_progression.scoring_note: A plan recommending only home bandaging without in-person wound closure is inadequate given the depth and gape of the wound.

red_flags.description: Deep, widely gaping wound with visible subcutaneous fat
red_flags.clinical_significance: Indicates need for formal wound closure rather than simple dressing.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed healing, increased infection risk, and worse scarring if not closed within the golden period.

red_flags.description: Bleeding not fully controlled despite firm pressure
red_flags.clinical_significance: Requires ongoing pressure and clinical haemostasis measures.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Continued blood loss if not properly addressed.

red_flags.description: Intact distal pulses, capillary refill, full range of motion against resistance, and intact two-point discrimination on examination (clinician-only findings)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Confirms no major tendon, nerve, or vascular injury.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Not directly available to the AI Doctor via chat, though patient-reported normal movement/sensation is a reasonable substitute pending formal exam.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Either over-triaging to an ambulance or under-triaging to simple home bandaging
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: AI Doctor should neither call an ambulance for a stable, controlled laceration nor dismiss it as manageable with a simple bandage — the correct action is prompt (same-day, ideally within a few hours) in-person assessment for formal wound closure.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent same-day in-person assessment (GP with procedural skills or ED) for wound exploration, irrigation, and suturing within the golden period; continue firm direct pressure and elevation in the meantime
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Recommending only home bandaging for a deep, gaping wound risks delayed closure, infection, and avoidable scarring.
